description: Observer delivery is per write, and `batch` does NOT suppress or coalesce it (#lzdartobservercow). Inside a batch, two writes to one cell MUST invoke each registration twice — once per write — not once at the outermost batch exit. This is the clause that distinguishes an observer from an Effect, and it is the reason both mechanisms exist: an Effect is batched, fires once when the cone settles, and is glitch-free; an observer bypasses that machinery deliberately and sees every intermediate transition, including the ones a batch was created to hide. A consumer that wants the settled value should use an Effect, which is cheaper and easier to reason about. A consumer that needs every transition — a mutation log, a replication feed, a persistence tap — cannot use an Effect at all, and coalescing observer delivery would defeat the only use case that justifies an out-of-band callback list. Note also that a pre-commit observer cannot be coalesced even in principle: if two writes land in one batch, there is no single value a `before_write` hook could meaningfully fire 'before'. The `==` store-guard still applies inside a batch: writing the value a cell already holds is not a write and MUST NOT notify. Assertions are on invocation counts only; nothing here inspects the collection or the batch implementation. Known to fail today against lazily-py, which routes notification through `batch.notify_change` and queues one coalesced touch at the outermost batch exit. lazily-dart, lazily-go, and lazily-zig invoke observers before consulting the batching machinery at all, and conform.

expect.note: baseline outside a batch: one write, one invocation per registration
the discriminating step — per-write delivery gives 4 invocations, coalesced delivery gives 2. Registration order holds within each write.
the `==` store-guard is not suspended by batch: the second write stores the value the cell already holds, so it is not a write and does not notify
nesting does not change it — delivery is per write at any batch depth, so a nested batch is not a coalescing boundary either
disposal composes with batching exactly as it does outside one
